title: Province Of Sindh through Chief Secretary, Government Of Sindh v. Abdul Sattar Khokhar
parties: Province Of Sindh through Chief Secretary, Government Of Sindh v. Abdul Sattar Khokhar
description:
Seniority/Challenge to seniority list

(1) Principle of “no aggrieved person, no appeal”:  doctrine of locus standi (2) The jurisdiction of Courts cannot be invoked for immaterial questions having no nexus with the valid grievance (3) Except Government, no individual employee whose seniority is affected has challenged the judgment (4) Government should act as a fair employer and not as a contestant fighting employees.

Bench: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar

Announced: 18-03-2026
keywords: Seniority/Challenge to seniority list, Judge: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar

parties: Muhammad Afzal v. Director GEO Information Center Survey of Pakistan, Peshawar and others
description:
Major Penalty / Termination from Service/Illegal/Irregular Appointment

(1) Decision “on the merits” means a judgment on substantial issues as binding decision under the doctrine of res judicata (2) If a Court/Tribunal lacks the jurisdiction, the finest option is to return the case (3) Any adjudication by a court or tribunal without jurisdiction is coram non judice and may prejudice right to a fair trial and due process.

title: Dr. Hassan Fatima v. Pakistan Red Crescent Society & others
parties: Dr. Hassan Fatima v. Pakistan Red Crescent Society & others
description:
Rent/Default in Payment of Rent

(1) Plea of old tenancy or investment for establishing business or earning goodwill is irrelevant (2) Statement on oath, if not shaken in cross-examination is sufficient to prove the bona fide for personal need (3) Default is considered wilful when it is found deliberate while technical default can be identified such as a negligible delay (4) Tender of rent is a legal obligation which cannot be escaped on account of flimsy pleas (5) Binding effect of MOU and nova
keywords: Rent/Default in Payment of Rent, Judge: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar
full_text:
Rent/Default in Payment of Rent

(1) Plea of old tenancy or investment for establishing business or earning goodwill is irrelevant (2) Statement on oath, if not shaken in cross-examination is sufficient to prove the bona fide for personal need (3) Default is considered wilful when it is found deliberate while technical default can be identified such as a negligible delay (4) Tender of rent is a legal obligation which cannot be escaped on account of flimsy pleas (5) Binding effect of MOU and novation of contract (6) If the landlord obtains the possession for personal need and relets the premises, the tenant may apply to the Rent Controller for restoration of possession.

Bench: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar

Announced: 23-12-2025

title: Ejaz Ahmed v. Israr-ul-Ebad Khan (Since Deceased) & others
parties: Ejaz Ahmed v. Israr-ul-Ebad Khan (Since Deceased) & others
description:
Rent/Ejectment

(1) The relationship between an advocate and his client is of principal and agent (2) The authority of advocate ceases on death of client (3) It is the professional duty of advocate to intimate the Court forthwith (4) Concealment of death is against the professional etiquettes (5) Advocate cannot continue without proper authorization of legal representatives of the deceased (6) If the explanation of advocate is found unreasonable, the Court may refer the matter to the concerned B
keywords: Rent/Ejectment, Judge: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar
full_text:
Rent/Ejectment

(1) The relationship between an advocate and his client is of principal and agent (2) The authority of advocate ceases on death of client (3) It is the professional duty of advocate to intimate the Court forthwith (4) Concealment of death is against the professional etiquettes (5) Advocate cannot continue without proper authorization of legal representatives of the deceased (6) If the explanation of advocate is found unreasonable, the Court may refer the matter to the concerned Bar Council to initiate misconduct proceedings.

Bench: Mr. Justice Muhammad Ali Mazhar

Announced: 19-12-2025

title: Federation of Pakistan through Secretary, Finance, Finance Division, Islamabad v. Farid Ullah and others
parties: Federation of Pakistan through Secretary, Finance, Finance Division, Islamabad v. Farid Ullah and others
description:
Increment/Qualification increment

The Supreme Court Rules, 2025 prescribe a period of limitation which is not just a technical requirement but has substantive importance. The State is required to adhere to statutory timeframes and cannot disregard the same on account of its own internal administrative movement of files, committee scheduling constraints, or staffing shortages which cannot be accepted as sufficient cause.

Bench: Mrs. Justice Ayesha A. Malik

title: Raja Khan v. Suhail Arif Shah and another
parties: Raja Khan v. Suhail Arif Shah and another
description:
Illegal Dispossession/Delivery of Possession of Property

Article 10-A of the Constitution guarantees the fundamental right to a fair trial and due process in the determination of criminal charges as well as civil rights and obligations. Under Article 187 of the Constitution, the Supreme Court possesses the authority to issue such directions, orders, or decrees as may be necessary for doing complete justice in any case or matter pending before it. This constitutional power obligates all executiv
keywords: Illegal Dispossession/Delivery of Possession of Property, Judge: Mr. Justice Jamal Khan Mandokhail
full_text:
Illegal Dispossession/Delivery of Possession of Property

Article 10-A of the Constitution guarantees the fundamental right to a fair trial and due process in the determination of criminal charges as well as civil rights and obligations. Under Article 187 of the Constitution, the Supreme Court possesses the authority to issue such directions, orders, or decrees as may be necessary for doing complete justice in any case or matter pending before it. This constitutional power obligates all executive and judicial authorities including the High Courts, throughout Pakistan to act upon the directions of the Supreme Court to ensure effective implementation of statutory mandates for the purpose of regulating judicial process.

Bench: Mr. Justice Jamal Khan Mandokhail

Announced: 25-05-2026
